source: Santa Clara Municipal Code Title 18 (Zoning) / General Plan Land Use Classifications / Patrick Henry Drive Specific Plan / Downtown Precise Plan / Lawrence Station Area Plan / Tasman East Specific Plan
notes: Density ranges are based on the 2024 Zoning Code Update (effective Aug 2024, updated July 2025), General Plan land use designations, and applicable Specific Plans. Zones with 'HT' suffix are Historic Combining District overlays (Ch. 18.58) that inherit the base zone's density. PD zones have variable density set by individual development plans. Patrick Henry (PH) Specific Plan zones (HDF, UC, UV, VR, PH-R5) are governed by the Patrick Henry Drive Specific Plan adopted March 2022. Downtown zones (DNTW, DTHT) are governed by the Downtown Precise Plan Form Based Code adopted December 2023.

CD.description: Downtown Commercial (Ch. 18.40) - Santa Clara Station Area

DNTW.description: Downtown (Form Based Code, Downtown Precise Plan) - Community Mixed Use GP designation; ~43 DU/AC average planned across 25-acre area with up to 1,071 units

DTHT.description: Downtown - Historic Combining (Form Based Code + Historic overlay Ch. 18.58) - Community Mixed Use GP designation; density same as DNTW base with historic preservation standards

HDF.description: High Density Flex (Patrick Henry Drive Specific Plan) - Office/Retail/Residential flex zone

HO-RD.description: High-Intensity Office/R&D (Ch. 18.46) - GP designation: High-Intensity Office/R&D

LO-RD.description: Low-Intensity Office/R&D (Ch. 18.48) - GP designation: Low-Intensity Office/R&D

LSAP.description: Lawrence Station Area Plan district - Mixed density subareas (Low 8-19, Medium 20-36, High 37-50, Very High 51-100 DU/AC); overall max 100 DU/AC

MH.description: Heavy Industrial (Ch. 18.50) - Santa Clara Station Area

MP.description: Planned Industrial (Ch. 18.46) - GP designation: High-Intensity Office/R&D

MUCC.description: Mixed Use - Community Commercial (Ch. 18.38) - Community Mixed Use GP designation; min 0.10 FAR commercial

MUCCHT.description: Mixed Use - Community Commercial - Historic Combining (Ch. 18.58 overlay) - Same density as MUCC base with historic preservation standards

MUNC.description: Mixed Use - Neighborhood Commercial (Ch. 18.34) - Neighborhood Mixed Use GP designation; min 0.10 FAR commercial; min 10 DU/AC for sites <1 acre, min 20 DU/AC for sites >1 acre

MURC.description: Mixed Use - Regional Commercial (Ch. 18.36) - Regional Mixed Use GP designation; min 0.15 FAR commercial; residential 37-50 DU/AC required

OS.description: Open Space (Ch. 18.52) - Parks/Open Space GP designation

PD.description: Planned Development (Ch. 18.54) - Variable density per approved development plan; GP designation varies by parcel

PD-MC.description: Planned Development - Master Community (Ch. 18.56) - Variable density per approved master community plan; Related Santa Clara project

PH-R5.description: Patrick Henry - Very High Density Residential (Patrick Henry Drive Specific Plan) - R5-equivalent zone within Patrick Henry planning area

R1-6L.description: Single-Family Residential, 6,000 sq ft min lot (Ch. 18.12) - Very Low Density Residential GP designation

R1-6LHT.description: Single-Family Residential, 6,000 sq ft min lot - Historic Combining (Ch. 18.12 + Ch. 18.58 overlay) - Same density as R1-6L base

R1-8L.description: Single-Family Residential, 8,000 sq ft min lot (Ch. 18.10) - Very Low Density Residential GP designation

R2.description: Low Density Residential / Duplex (Ch. 18.14) - Very Low to Low Density Residential GP designation

R2-7L.description: Low Density Residential, 7,000 sq ft min lot (Ch. 18.14) - GP designation: High Density Residential (site-specific)

R2HT.description: Low Density Residential - Historic Combining (Ch. 18.14 + Ch. 18.58 overlay) - Same density as R2 base

R3.description: Medium Density Residential (Ch. 18.18) - Medium Density Residential GP designation; townhouses, low-rise apartments

R4.description: High Density Residential (Ch. 18.48) - GP designation: High Density Residential; mid-rise buildings, structured parking

TN.description: Transit Neighborhood (Ch. 18.25, Tasman East Specific Plan) - Min 60 DU/AC on parcels <1 acre (pre-Nov 2018), min 100 DU/AC on all other parcels; max 350 DU/AC; max height 220 ft

UC.description: Urban Center (Patrick Henry Drive Specific Plan) - Highest density zone; buildings up to 25 stories

UV.description: Urban Village (Patrick Henry Drive Specific Plan) - High density mixed-use zone

VHDM.description: Very High Density Mixed Use (Ch. 18.22.210) - Transit/pedestrian-oriented mixed use; very high density housing with commercial uses

VHDR.description: Very High Density Residential (Ch. 18.42) - GP designation: High-Intensity Office/R&D area; R5-equivalent zone

VR.description: Village Residential (Patrick Henry Drive Specific Plan) - Residential-focused zone within Patrick Henry area
